texto: Sometimes it's better to pretend as if you know nothing. The vast majority of people can't be trusted, but in God I trust.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a S.Z.J. Mtshali: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de passagem do tempo e prioridades. O trecho “Sometimes it's better to pretend as if you know nothing. The vast majority of people can't be tru…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que reflexao, trust, sometimes pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de termino e reflexao.

frase_original: Sometimes it's better to pretend as if you know nothing. The vast majority of people can't be trusted, but in God I trust.

texto: Moving on is the hardest thing to do, when that person made your life worth living for. Nomatter how difficult it's but we have to let go for own sake. Remember that your life is more precious than anything in this world.
explicacao: Orientação direta de S.Z.J. Mtshali sobre sentido da existência e experiência vivida. A afirmação “Moving on is the hardest thing to do, when that person made your life worth living for. Nomatter …” funciona como lembrete prático ligado a reflexao, moving-on, moving, útil em momentos de reflexao.
